candidates.oracle: ref_result == compare_result (exact token/text equality across PP vs TP)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES: exact text/token equality across PP vs TP configs, no tolerance
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES: PyTorch numeric changes in attention/matmul could change generated tokens
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES: no contract guarantees PP and TP-only produce identical tokens; VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT not set

candidates.comparison: cosine similarity >= 0.999 for embeddings, exact equality for other fields
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES: 0.999 cosine similarity is near-exact; small numeric drift breaks it
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: YES: PyTorch changes in embedding computation could shift vectors below 0.999
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: YES: no contract guarantees PP and TP-only produce identical embeddings
